title: World Cup Bar Crawl Walkability Assessment
subtitle: Somerville, MA — FIFA World Cup 2026 Fan Route

context.city_transit_score: Top 10 nationally (City of Somerville official recognition)

route.type: Irish pub — flagship Davis Square institution, live traditional Irish music nightly, known as a World Cup soccer hub
route.address: 247 Elm Street, Davis Square, Somerville, MA 02144
route.geocode_display: The Burren, 247, Elm Street, Davis Square, Ball Square, Somerville

infrastructure_assessment.data_note: Geocoded to project boundary; spatial query returned no pavement records at this exact bbox — imagery data available
infrastructure_assessment.nearby_pci_context: Surrounding Davis Square streets show mix of Good (97–98 PCI) and Poor (43–53 PCI) segments within 200m radius; Elm Street itself is well-maintained

route.walkability_notes: Davis Square T stop (Red Line) directly adjacent. Elm Street is a pedestrian-friendly commercial corridor with wide sidewalks, dedicated crosswalks, and active street lighting. High foot traffic zone — ideal crawl start.
route.world_cup_angle: Known soccer broadcast venue; Irish pubs traditionally draw Republic of Ireland fans; likely to show all group stage matches

route.type: Upscale American bar, extensive whiskey and cocktail selection, large-screen TVs
route.address: 255 Elm Street, Davis Square, Somerville, MA 02144
route.geocode_display: Saloon, 255, Elm Street, Davis Square, Ball Square, Somerville
route.walkability_notes: Directly across from The Burren — 30 foot walk. Same Elm Street corridor. Excellent street lighting from Davis Square commercial district. Red Line T stop within 50 meters.
route.world_cup_angle: Upscale watch party venue; reservations likely needed for marquee matches

route.type: Craft beer bar — 40 draft lines, 120+ bottles, signature cocktails. Walk Score award winner.
route.address: 256 Elm Street, Davis Square, Somerville, MA 02144
route.geocode_display: Elm Street Taproom, 256, Elm Street, Davis Square, Ball Square, Somerville

infrastructure_assessment.key_imagery_findings: Imagery at lat 42.3937, lon -71.1210 shows active pedestrian presence with traffic light, bus, fire hydrant — classic Davis Square intersection

route.walkability_notes: Across street from Saloon — under 50m. This three-bar Davis Square cluster (Burren, Saloon, Elm St Taproom) can be walked in under 2 minutes total. Red Line Davis station is the anchor.
route.world_cup_angle: Best beer selection on the route for extended match watching; craft breweries sponsoring World Cup tie-ins

route.type: Gastropub — 37 drafts, 150+ bourbons/whiskies, sports bar atmosphere, multiple large screens
route.address: 400 Highland Avenue, Davis Square, Somerville, MA 02144
route.geocode_display: 400, Highland Avenue, Davis Square, Ball Square, Somerville

infrastructure_assessment.key_note: Highland Ave is a primary arterial — wider lanes but active crosswalks and pedestrian signals

route.walkability_notes: 540m walk from Elm Street Taproom via Elm St and Holland St. Mostly flat, well-lit commercial corridor. Crosses Highland Ave — signal-controlled intersection. Some sidewalk patching noted on this stretch.
route.world_cup_angle: Dedicated sports bar with most screens — ideal for knockout round matches; bourbon flight specials likely

route.type: Hidden craft cocktail bar — nearly unmarked door, rotating thematic menus, serious mixology. Among top cocktail bars in greater Boston.
route.address: 5 Sanborn Court (off Bow Street), Union Square, Somerville, MA 02143
route.geocode_display: Backbar, 5, Sanborn Court, Union Square, Somerville

infrastructure_assessment.key_note: Union Square area shows slightly more wear than Davis Square — more Fair/Satisfactory segments; one Poor segment at 45.29 PCI near Washington St

route.walkability_notes: This is the longest segment — 1.85km from Davis Square to Union Square, approximately 22 min walk via Elm St -> Summer St -> Union Square. Green Line Extension (GLX) Union Square station now operational, making this segment transit-optional. The route passes through a mix of residential and commercial blocks.
route.transit_option: Green Line Extension D-branch: Davis to Union Square via 1 transfer OR bus 86/87
route.world_cup_angle: Sophistication stop — World Cup-themed cocktail menu highly likely; intimate setting ideal for quarterfinal celebrations

route.type: Classic neighborhood bar-restaurant, extensive tap list, longtime Union Square anchor since 1997

infrastructure_assessment.data_note: Spatial query returned no pavement records — Union Square plaza area may fall in gap in pavement dataset coverage
infrastructure_assessment.nearby_pci_context: Surrounding blocks (Washington St, Bow St) predominantly Satisfactory (70–84 PCI) with some Fair segments; Union Square itself recently redeveloped with new pavement

route.walkability_notes: 180m from backbar within Union Square plaza. Union Square underwent major $50M streetscape reconstruction completed 2022-2023 — new sidewalks, protected bike lanes, plaza improvements, and new GLX station. Excellent pedestrian infrastructure at this stop.
route.world_cup_angle: The neighborhood institution — where locals watch; expect full house for USA or Portugal matches

route.type: Central European beer hall — sausage, pretzels, extensive German/Czech/Belgian beer selection. Natural soccer atmosphere.
route.address: 255 Washington Street, Union Square, Somerville, MA 02143
route.geocode_display: Bronwyn, 255, Washington Street, Prospect Hill, East Somerville, Somerville

concern_segments.label: Poor
concern_segments.note: One segment on Washington St corridor needs attention

concern_segments.label: Poor
concern_segments.note: Secondary segment near Prospect Hill area

route.walkability_notes: 60m from The Independent — same block. Washington Street has active pedestrian traffic day and night. Note: one Poor-condition pavement segment (PCI 45.29) detected on this block — potential trip hazard for evening foot traffic. Sidewalks on Washington St recently improved as part of Union Square redevelopment.
route.world_cup_angle: German beer hall = European soccer culture. Expect Bayern/Bundesliga regulars; World Cup bracket parties; ideal semifinal venue

route_segments.pavement_condition: Good to Satisfactory (PCI 85-98 on this stretch)

route_segments.description: South on Elm St, east on Holland St to Highland Ave
route_segments.pavement_condition: Mixed — Good on Elm St (PCI 91-98), declining to Fair/Poor on connecting streets (PCI 44-68)
hazards: Poor PCI segment (43.91) detected on side street
Patching and light cracking on Holland St connecting blocks
Highland Ave is 4-lane arterial — use signal

route_segments.description: Longest segment — east on Highland Ave, south on Willow Ave, east on Somerville Ave to Union Square. OR: Green Line Extension Davis -> Union Square
route_segments.pavement_condition: Variable — Fair to Satisfactory (PCI 54-84) along Somerville Ave corridor
hazards: Poor pavement segment (PCI 53.96) detected near route at lat 42.3772, lon -71.0971
Block cracking and sealant distress along sections of Somerville Ave
Night walking on arterial — use caution

transit_option.route: Green Line Extension (E branch toward Union Square) or MBTA Bus 86/87

route_segments.pavement_condition: Satisfactory with one Poor segment (PCI 45.29 flagged)
hazards: One Poor-condition pavement segment (PCI 45.29) on Washington St — potential trip hazard at night
Depressions and bumps/sags detected in pavement distress data

davis_square_cluster.description: Walkable in under 2 minutes total — effectively one location

inter_cluster_connector.recommendation: Consider MBTA Green Line Extension to break up the route

crash_data_nearby.source: Vision Zero Crash Records — City of Boston
crash_data_nearby.note: Dataset covers Boston proper — Somerville is an independent city; crash data shown is for the broader Metro Boston area to establish context. Somerville crash data available separately via data.somervillema.gov

somerville_specific_context.vision_zero_status: Somerville has its own Vision Zero program — zero fatality commitment
somerville_specific_context.somerville_pedestrian_count_program: Annual volunteer count since 2010 — comprehensive ped/bike activity data

world_cup_context.tournament: 2026 FIFA World Cup — United States, Canada, Mexico co-hosts
world_cup_context.venue: Gillette Stadium, Foxborough, MA (30 miles south of Somerville)
world_cup_context.match_times: Kickoffs typically 12pm, 3pm, 6pm ET for group stage; evening for knockouts
world_cup_context.expected_fans: Foxborough hosting 7 matches; Boston-area hotel capacity strained
world_cup_context.somerville_as_base: Somerville is 6.5 miles north of Boston's city center, serviced by Red Line and GLX — natural fan accommodation zone

watch_party_infrastructure.city_of_cambridge: 7 free outdoor watch parties planned (adjacent city, receiving state grants)
watch_party_infrastructure.mass_grant_program: Massachusetts spending $10M on World Cup watch parties statewide
watch_party_infrastructure.fifa_fan_festival: Boston City Hall Plaza — free tournament-long activation with giant screens
watch_party_infrastructure.bar_count_somerville_estimate: Approximately 30+ licensed bars in Davis Square and Union Square combined

key_storylines_for_demo: USA home crowd — massive domestic interest
European teams (Germany, Netherlands, Portugal, England) with large Somerville expat communities
Irish-American population in Davis Square — Republic of Ireland qualification pending
Brazilian and Latin American communities in East Somerville near Union Square

permit_requirements.jurisdiction: Massachusetts Alcoholic Beverages Control Commission (ABCC) + local licensing boards
permit_requirements.standard_hours: Last call 2:00 AM Massachusetts state law
permit_requirements.extended_hours_status: Massachusetts has NOT enacted World Cup-specific extended hours legislation (as of April 2026). Compare: Missouri enacted automatic sunset extension through July 20; British Columbia allowing extended service hours for FIFA licensees June 11 – July 19.
permit_requirements.somerville_licensing: Somerville License Commission governs local liquor license conditions — special event applications available
permit_requirements.foxborough_status: Foxborough itself delayed granting World Cup operating license pending public safety funding commitment (per WBUR March 2026 report)
recommendations_for_bars: Apply to Somerville License Commission for special event hours variance (World Cup match days)
Coordinate with ABCC for temporary license modifications
Outdoor seating permits may require separate application for expanded World Cup capacity
Security staffing requirements increase with capacity
permit_requirements.occupancy_considerations: Bars expecting 200%+ normal capacity on USA match days — fire code compliance and crowd management planning essential

overall_walkability_score.basis: Somerville official Walk Score 89/100; Cyvl infrastructure data showing predominantly Good-to-Satisfactory pavement conditions on main route; dense pedestrian infrastructure (852 push buttons, 619 traffic signals, 3,274 sidewalk assets citywide); Red Line and GLX transit connectivity
strengths: Davis Square cluster is world-class walkable — 3 bars within 75 meters
Union Square cluster excellent — recently reconstructed streetscape (2022-23)
Red Line and GLX provide transit relief for the 1.85km inter-cluster segment
High density of pedestrian signals and push buttons (852 citywide)
Mostly flat terrain — no significant grade changes on route
Active commercial corridors with natural evening lighting
concerns: One Poor pavement segment (PCI 43.91) detected near Davis Square cluster — potential nighttime trip hazard
Several Poor segments (PCI 44-53) scattered along Five Horses Tavern vicinity
One Poor segment (PCI 45.29) on Washington Street in Union Square cluster — depressions and bumps noted
Highland Avenue crossing between Davis Square and Five Horses Tavern is a 4-lane arterial
1.85km Davis-to-Union-Square walk is the weak link — transit use recommended
Night-time pedestrian safety: 104 CCTV cameras citywide but bar district coverage unknown
overall_walkability_score.recommendation: Route is highly suitable for World Cup bar crawl. Recommend: (1) Start at Davis Square cluster (3 bars, minimal walking); (2) Five Horses Tavern as 4th stop adds 540m; (3) Use GLX or bus for Davis-to-Union-Square connector; (4) Finish Union Square cluster (3 bars, 240m total). Highlight Poor pavement segments to city as pre-World Cup repair priorities.

cyvl_demo_highlights.headline_stat: Cyvl has catalogued 21,141 road segments and 51,000+ infrastructure assets in Somerville — giving the city unprecedented visibility into pedestrian safety before the World Cup arrives

poor_segments_needing_repair.call_to_action: With 7 World Cup matches drawing 500,000+ fans through Gillette Stadium 30 miles away, Somerville has 46.7 miles of poor-or-worse pavement to address before June 13

accessible_infrastructure.note: ADA compliance critical for international fan base

cyvl_demo_highlights.imagery_advantage: 34 imagery data points around Davis Square alone — city can remotely assess every sidewalk crack before fans arrive
